discovery.text: From Allan B. Jacobs' Great Streets (1993). One of Jacobs' American examples that works — the Mag Mile as a case study in how American cities can build streets with genuine presence.

narrative.summary: One of Jacobs' American examples that works — the Mag Mile as a case study in how American cities can build streets with genuine presence.

blocks.title: Why Jacobs Chose It
blocks.body: Wide sidewalks, continuous active street edges, the Chicago River crossing, major architectural landmarks (Tribune Tower, Wrigley Building, John Hancock). The gap between this and most American commercial streets is enormous — Jacobs explains why.

blocks.title: How to Walk It
blocks.body: Walk the full length. Jacobs is explicit: you have to walk a great street to understand it — the sequence, the proportions, how the edges change. Give it an hour minimum. Sit at a café or on a bench midway. Notice what makes it feel alive.
